name: diolog-plugins
description: DiologIR Claude Code plugins for product discovery and investor relations workflows

plugins.name: positioning-pipeline
plugins.description: End-to-end product-positioning pipeline. From a company context it generates two Google Gemini Deep Research prompts (a positioning brief + a product-research-persona market brief), renders a copy-and-launch HTML with animated Gemini instructions (select Deep research from the + menu; Share & Export → Copy contents), then — once the research comes back — synthesises three framework-grounded positioning territories (Ries & Trout, April Dunford, Blue Ocean, David C. Baker) as Markdown plus a stylised interactive decision page.

plugins.name: discovery-sentinel
plugins.description: Analyze product discovery and customer feedback session documents using the Discovery Sentinel persona. Types sessions by purpose (discovery vs demo), segments distinct client companies, and produces lean Feedback Classification Reports, Discovery Insight Briefs, readable Prioritised Opportunity Assessments, an Action Checklist, and company-specific Sales Discovery Companions — with confidence scoring, JTBD extraction, bias detection, and compliance flagging for regulated B2B SaaS. Includes a trend-synthesis mode across prior analyses.

plugins.name: product-strategist
plugins.description: Generate validated, prioritized product feature concepts and outcome-based roadmaps for AI-assisted IR/compliance/governance SaaS. Supports feature ideation, roadmap construction, feature evaluation, data ingestion, and comparative prioritization using RICE-C/WSJF scoring, compliance gates, and OST methodology.

plugins.name: feedback-archivist
plugins.description: Turn messy multi-source customer-discovery/feedback dumps (Slack exports, Google-Drive "Notes by Gemini" .docx meeting notes, segment-foldered research/ICPs/personas, AI-Studio JSON repos, screenshot evidence, audio/video) into ONE clean, deduplicated, verbatim Markdown archive organised by buyer segment with an INDEX — the pre-step that feeds discovery-sentinel and product-strategist. Converts via pandoc, classifies meetings by attendee email domain + source folder, dedupes identical content + notes/transcript pairs + Slack recaps, mines Slack for product feedback (dropping bots/internal-eng/test noise), strips Gemini auto-summaries only where a transcript exists, and builds a who's-who INDEX with provenance and probable dates.

plugins.name: deep-research-prompt-creator
plugins.description: Turns a vague research need into a single copy-paste-ready Google Gemini 3.1 Pro Deep Research prompt — with pseudo-XML scaffolding, archetype-specific overrides (technical / competitive / regulatory / academic / forecasting), epistemic bounding tags, inline citation protocol, and wrap-around Operator Notes (Plan Review, decomposition, adversarial audit) that empirically matter as much as the prompt itself.

plugins.name: code-review
plugins.description: High-signal code review for NestJS, Next.js (App Router)/React 19, frontend HTML/CSS/React, and React Native. Depth levels (quick/standard/deep) scale token cost; area targeting (frontend, nest, next, mobile, paths) and focus lenses (bugs, security, perf, tests, components, a11y, dead-code, debt, deps, dx) scope each run; a token-light prepush gate scans the outgoing diff. Modular checklist routing, mitigating-controls auto-discovery, parallel sharding for large diffs, independent batched Sonnet verifier fan-out with five gates (API existence, versions, mitigation-elsewhere, proportionality, reachability), and cross-run learned suppressions. Quality lenses adapted from shadcn's improve skill (MIT); noise-reduction patterns from 2025-26 AppSec research (Corgea, Aikido, ZeroPath).

plugins.name: design-md-from-screenshots
plugins.description: Reverse-engineer a product's visual design system from one or more website/app screenshots and produce a single authoritative DESIGN.md — a framework-agnostic, token-based spec (palette, typography, spacing, shape, motion, component inventory, voice, do/don't) with per-token provenance marks, so another AI (Stitch, Claude, v0, Cursor, AI Elements) can generate new screens that unmistakably belong to the same product.

plugins.name: doc-summarizer
plugins.description: Compress a verbose source document — README, API docs, research paper, technical guide, library docs — into a maximally dense, semantically complete Markdown reference (written to a `<source>.summary.md` file) that another LLM or a skimming human can consume in a fraction of the tokens. Uses a triage-tag system ([CRITICAL]/[WORKFLOW]/[POWER-USER]/[GOLDEN-NUGGET]/[DEPRECATED]), contextually integrated tips, solution-first problem framing, multi-tier feature capture, and a mandatory Strategic Synthesis footer (Maturity Model + Feature Selection Matrix + Workflow Progression Ladder). Target compression 60–80% while retaining 100% of semantically unique information.

plugins.name: presentation-design-md-from-screenshots
plugins.description: Reverse-engineer a slide deck's visual design system from one or more slide screenshots and produce a single authoritative PRESENTATION_DESIGN.md — a tool-agnostic, token-based spec (canvas, palette, typography, slide archetypes, chart style, image treatment, iconography, motion, do/don't) hard-capped at 1,700 characters so another AI (Gamma, Beautiful.ai, Pitch, Keynote/PowerPoint co-pilots, Stitch, v0, Claude, Cursor) can generate new slides that unmistakably belong to the same deck. Sibling to design-md-from-screenshots but tuned for slides: fixed 16:9 canvas, slide archetypes, chart data-ink discipline, image treatment conventions, and on-slide positional grammar.

plugins.name: create-persona
plugins.description: Generates dense, agent-ready persona definitions from a target role — identity kernel, responsibility matrix with classification tags ([CRITICAL] / [WORKFLOW] / [POWER-USER] / [GOLDEN-NUGGET]), decision frameworks, maturity model, selection matrix, capability heat map, dependency graph, metrics, constraints, and two interaction examples. Tuned to current Claude model behaviour (literalism, adaptive thinking, positive framing). Evidence base from 2025 persona research (EmoCharacter, LABE, PPA, CPER, Over-Specification Paradox) ships in references/playbook.md.

plugins.name: log-bugs-to-diolog-tasks
plugins.description: Create Diolog Tasks issues from a code-review markdown report. Each `### [SEVERITY] Title` finding becomes one issue with severity (Critical/High/Medium/Low) and type (Vulnerability/Bug) labels and Todo status. Formerly log-bugs-to-linear; now targets the diolog-tasks MCP. Preserves the reviewer's exact prose, file:line citations, fix recommendations, and confidence scores.

plugins.name: diolog-tasks-pipeline
plugins.description: Three native skills that run the Diolog Tasks issue pipeline — triage, plan, and worker — entirely inside your interactive Claude Code session (no Agent SDK, so usage draws from your standard interactive allowance, not the separate Agent SDK credit). Formerly linear-issue-pipeline; now targets the diolog-tasks MCP (mcp.diolog.com.au). tasks-triage runs a technical + Specification Sentinel product review and posts a non-technical readiness comment; tasks-plan classifies a tier, investigates the codebase, writes docs/plans/{id}.md and comments the repo-relative path (no file upload); tasks-worker implements a planned issue in an isolated worktree via dynamic ultracode workflows (understand → implement → rebase → acceptance review → resolve), with no remote PR. The plan and worker fan out parallel subagents via the Workflow tool to speed up large investigations.

plugins.name: feature-spec-pipeline
plugins.description: Four native skills that run an issue-tracker-free, markdown-doc feature pipeline — /triage, /plan, /work, /gap-fix — entirely inside your interactive Claude Code session. /triage turns a feature idea (inline text or a notes file) into a versioned spec at docs/specs/spec-DIO-0001.md (id allocated from docs/feature-specs/LEDGER.md), running a codebase grounding pass plus a Specification Sentinel product/UX/compliance review and appending a non-technical readiness section. /plan classifies a tier, investigates the codebase, writes docs/plans/plan-DIO-0001.md and links it from the spec. /work implements the plan in an isolated worktree via dynamic ultracode workflows (understand → implement → rebase → acceptance review → resolve), appends a progress section to the spec, and leaves the branch local — no remote PR. /gap-fix runs after /work as the remediation step: it re-enters the work branch, ALWAYS re-audits the delivered code against the original spec (merging in any human/QA gaps you provide), and implements the fixes in code (file-disjoint fan-out + typecheck gates), looping audit→fix until only optional Low items remain, then commits locally and appends a gap-fix progress note — no remote PR. The plan, work, and gap-fix skills fan out parallel subagents via the Workflow tool (in small batches to avoid throttling). A doc-driven sibling of diolog-tasks-pipeline that needs no Diolog Tasks MCP.

plugins.name: marketing-docs-maintenance
plugins.description: Keep the Diolog marketing/feature documentation set in the diolog-team-files repo (~/Dev/diolog-team-files/web) current when a feature ships or a Diolog Tasks ticket lands. Updates the two assembled published docs — existing-features-technical.md (full technical detail) and exhisting-features-marketing.md (plain, non-technical) — plus the per-area detail under existing-features-references/ (final/ + plain/ numbered per area 01-22, backed by the live/ and raw/ capture layers), and the auxiliary surfaces outbound-contact-surfaces.md, ui-interaction-details.md, and INCORPORATED.md (reconciliation ledger). Enforces the content standards (technical files carry component names, routes, GraphQL ops and exact copy with no opinions; plain files carry zero technical terms, second person, sentence case, no em dashes, no emojis), pair consistency (each assembled doc agrees with its per-area source), the supersede-don't-accumulate currency rule, and the live-app > source > ticket source-of-truth hierarchy. Operationalises docs/marketing/MAINTENANCE.md (the canonical standards guide in the app repo), with the published docs now living in diolog-team-files/web.

plugins.name: spec-validation
plugins.description: Validate whether a specification, build plan, or Definition of Done is GENUINELY implemented in a large codebase — not just rendered, schema-valid, and passing tests. Classifies every claimed-done data field as REAL (a producer computes it from the filesystem / git / discovery / AI / a live API), AUTHORED (read from persisted state but only ever written by a UI round-trip or a seed — nothing computes it), or MOCK (only ever a fallback constant), each with file:line evidence. Fans the work out across parallel investigators, adversarially verifies the highest-impact findings before reporting, and writes a ranked, date-stamped living gap report that doubles as the resolution backlog. Built to catch demo-data-dressed-as-done and to re-validate after a prior '100% complete' claim — the core insight being that renders + schema-valid + tests-pass never proves a real producer exists. Audit-only: it changes no product code — the gap-fix skill (feature-spec-pipeline) is the fixer.

plugins.name: design-craft
plugins.description: Turn Claude into an opinionated, accessibility-aware, AI-slop-resistant design collaborator that produces intentional design artifacts in HTML/CSS/SVG/JS — landing pages, interactive prototypes, mobile app screens, slide decks, wireframes, hi-fi variations, design-token extractions, and component inventories. The SKILL.md carries a full design philosophy (content discipline, purposeful aesthetics that reject AI-template tropes, visual hierarchy and rhythm, typography and color systems, interface copy as design material, WCAG accessibility, interaction states, system thinking, respecting the medium) and routes to 28 phased procedures across production (discovery-questions, frontend-aesthetic-direction, wireframe, make-a-deck, make-a-prototype, make-tweakable, generate-variations, make-an-animation, make-a-doc, generate-images), craft (motion-design, gsap-motion, depth-and-3d, laws-of-composition, typesetting, data-viz, mobile-design), system (design-system-extract, component-extract, design-system-author), and review (unit-critique-gate, accessibility-audit, ai-slop-check, hierarchy-rhythm-review, interaction-states-pass, visual-verification, polish-pass). Built for Claude Code: AskUserQuestion for kickoff rounds, the Agent tool for parallel verifier fan-out, and self-contained deck-scaling / device-frame / tweak-panel code that runs as standalone files. v1.2 adds mobile-design (iOS/Material platform grammar, thumb zone, input methods, industry conventions, emotional design), gsap-motion (timelines, ScrollTrigger scroll storytelling, SplitText, SVG draw/morph, Flip/Draggable — all plugins free via CDN), visual-verification (viewport-matrix layout integrity + screenshot playbook, a fifth polish-pass reviewer), an interface-copy chapter, subject-derived aesthetics with a plan-time signature element and swap-test genericness gate, and detection for all three current AI-default looks (warm-editorial, dark+acid-accent, broadsheet). v1.3 wires in local design-system libraries (open-design DESIGN.md format: brand systems as binding context, library systems as direction candidates, deck-template styles for deck variety), portable 9-section DESIGN.md emission from design-system-extract/author plus signature-moves capture, accent budgets written as enforceable usage rules, Karpathy-style surgical iteration discipline, and an interface-copy lens in the polish-pass jury. v1.4 adds an agentic-iteration layer — unit-critique-gate (a mid-build per-unit draft→critique→repair loop with a canonical scores+mustFix rubric, convergence budgets, and a don't-double-loop rule for orchestrated harnesses), a deterministic design-lint script (stdlib Python, runs in any seeded sandbox), an autonomous mode for orchestrated/headless runs (assumptions instead of questions, iterate harder), and per-unit visual micro-checks with honest degradation when no browser exists — plus the typesetting and data-viz craft references and the ux-craft companion skill as a standing dependency for flows, forms, and UX review. v1.5 adds a redesign procedure (references/redesign.md: preserve/overhaul mode detection, audit-before-touching incl. SEO/analytics dependencies, a never-change-silently list, and modernisation levers in priority order), optical/cross-card alignment checks in hierarchy-rhythm-review, logo-wall sourcing (Simple Icons / SVG monograms, logos-only) and bento background-diversity rules in ai-slop-check, the double-bezel nested-enclosure technique in depth-and-3d, and a deliver-the-whole-count rule for multi-unit briefs — selected synthesis from the taste-skill corpus. v1.6 hardens verification into an epistemics rather than a checklist: visual-verification gains a Phase 0 verification contract (rendering a screenshot is not seeing one; ask each capture "what is wrong with this?" not "is this done?"; a gate is downstream of the findings that motivated it, so a clean lint means no known defect is present and never means verified; prove every new rule can fail by running it against the artifact that motivated it; coverage is silent because a selector matching nothing passes; crop to components at DPR 2-3 because a page thumbnail cannot resolve the defect; measure ink via a baseline probe plus canvas TextMetrics.actualBoundingBoxAscent rather than getBoundingClientRect boxes; and static checks are structurally blind to motion), a three-line report shape separating what a machine asserts from what you looked at from what you did not check, make-a-doc's print freeze reworked around the resting-style-is-final-style contract (invert the states when you own the CSS; the negative-delay skip is the fallback for stylesheets you don't), motion-design's review gaining the three-pass motion verification (print at rest, reduced-motion at rest, mid-flight frames) and an explicit z-index rule for transient overlays, and unit-critique-gate spelling out that a clean lint licenses one sentence and it is not "verified".

plugins.name: email-mockups
plugins.description: Turn a named Diolog feature (or several) into email-ready product-mockup graphics on a Figma canvas. Reads the feature's real context — docs/marketing/product-feature-guide.md + features-build/plain/NN-*.md + the rendered mock UI read in a browser via playwright-cli (the served web / customer / investor design-system hosts) — then designs and builds, via the design-craft skill, impression-not-replica mockups that lead with the feature's payoff (a full product-window app shot — the complete app shell in a clean window — for hero features, plus a product-surface vignette, soft panel + peeking device, collage/overlap, phone bezel, and a website frame for actual public sites) and make the product look genuinely smart (a real insight, an italic quote from the record, named source chips) as titled, spaced artboards in one self-contained HTML file using the live design tokens, and recreates that layout in Figma via whatever write-capable Figma MCP is connected. Each artboard also carries a brief non-technical marketing caption beneath it for IR teams and the agencies who serve them (no em-dashes), with an optional email-hero split format, and semantic emphasis via a soft wash and icon rather than AI-slop left-only borders. If no Figma MCP is present, the HTML file is the final deliverable.

plugins.name: macosify
plugins.description: Three macOS-native UI skills sharing one bundled knowledge corpus (the full macOS 26 'Tahoe'/Liquid Glass HIG library + a token-level DESIGN.md + the macOS 26 UI Extraction & Native Translation framework). `macosify` refits an existing UI to feel authentically AppKit-native on macOS 26, correcting iOS-derived looks (Mac Catalyst / 'Designed for iPad' on Apple Silicon) toward true native. `macos-ui-analyst` (the GlassReader persona) analyses a static macOS 26 screenshot into a structured Native Translation Report (AX-tree inventory + SwiftUI/AppKit mapping) and merges durable learnings into one accumulating knowledge file. `macos-storyboard` reads the entire corpus, then builds an interactive StyleX + Storybook storyboard of a specification's user flows — a reusable design system (tokens → elements → composites) with every screen rendered across happy / empty / loading / error / AI states, using the design-craft and frontend-design skills for bespoke, accessible direction.

plugins.name: create-storyboard
plugins.description: Build an interactive UI storyboard as a StyleX + Storybook project from a product specification and a user-supplied DESIGN.md. The DESIGN.md (the product's own brand/design system) drives the look — this skill is NOT locked to a macOS aesthetic and renders in whatever visual identity the brand specifies. It reads a bundled HIG library purely as the interaction, behaviour, structure, and accessibility backbone (layout, master–detail/list/table grammar, modality, feedback, focus/keyboard, motion, empty/loading/error/status, writing), applies the design-craft and frontend-design skills for distinctive, accessible, AI-slop-resistant direction, derives the user flows + screens, builds a reusable StyleX design system (tokens → elements → composites) from the DESIGN.md, and implements every screen as Storybook stories across the happy / empty / loading / error / AI states at production fidelity, plus an MDX flow-overview that sequences the screens into journeys. A brand-driven sibling of macosify's macos-storyboard skill (which is locked to a macOS-native look).

plugins.name: mockup-fidelity
plugins.description: Validate that an implemented React or React Native UI faithfully reproduces a reference mockup (HTML+CSS, served prototype, Figma export, or another rendered component set), then update the code to close every gap — by measuring the rendered tree, never by eyeballing or reading source. Treats the mock as the source of truth, builds a COMPLETE screen/frame inventory (never silently drops a frame), extracts both sides to on-disk artifacts and generates the ledger from them, inverts the burden of proof (a visible difference is a defect until a citation proves it intentional), diffs structure-first then per-property computed styles, and produces a per-screen present/divergent/absent ledger plus a FUNCTIONAL-GAPS document for any newly-added UI that is only visual (missing backend, data wiring, endpoints). For React Native it measures the RENDERED tree — structural/accessibility extraction (axe describe-ui / Maestro hierarchy) plus resolved style props over the Metro CDP connection (rozenite / agent-cdp) — and forces every screen's measurement to disk as artifacts, so a 'match' verdict can never come from a screenshot, a source read, or reasoning (frontier vision models catch under half of fine-grained UI differences; an artifact-forcing gate plus a completeness-critic close that hole). For a React web target the comparison is symmetric DOM-to-DOM — reference and target are both read via getComputedStyle through one extractor at the same viewport, with the app chrome (sidebar/header/nav) in scope. Asks the user when unsure, on both a full-comparison and a per-screen basis. Generic and framework-agnostic — nothing app-specific.
plugins._v251note: 2.5.1 adds a deterministic TYPOGRAPHIC-DECLARATION check (class font) to the single-script analyzer/differ (assets/diff/analyze.js), closing the 'same font, different declared SHAPING' class that slipped every layer on the diolog.site↔diolog.app run: a button rendered the SINGLE-story 'a' (Inter font-feature-settings:cv11) where live is normal (double-story), and elsewhere the font-family DECLARATION differed (target 'Inter', system-ui, …, sans-serif vs live Inter, sans-serif). BOTH slipped: the computed-style differ compares font-family only by KIND (serif/sans/mono) and never looks at font-feature-settings; CDP getPlatformFontsForNode reports the SAME font FILE because a cvXX glyph is a variant WITHIN the file; and the odiff raster runs antialiasing:true so a glyph-edge variant is suppressed as smoothing noise. Per PAIRED text element MODE B now runs three exact comparisons: (1) font/feature-settings — normalized getComputedStyle.fontFeatureSettings (lowercase, strip quotes, sort the comma list, collapse whitespace, ''→normal); (2) font/variation-settings — same treatment for fontVariationSettings (a wght/opsz axis applied on one side only); (3) font/family-exact — the FULL fontFamily declaration normalized, complementing (not replacing) the existing kind check. Because these props are almost always set site-wide, each is DEDUPED per (property|normalizedTarget|normalizedRef) to ONE root-cause finding + a [×N elements] count (a whole-site cv11 or stack difference = ONE finding, not hundreds). MED severity; same text-probe scoping as the other font checks. Validated against controlled fixtures via the real analyze.js MODE A→B flow: (a) cv11 vs normal fires 1 deduped finding; (b) long system stack vs Inter, sans-serif fires 1; (c) a font-variation-settings divergence fires; (d) identical pages = 0 new findings; (e) equivalent-but-different-syntax declarations (reordered/requoted) = 0 (normalization does not false-positive).
plugins._v25note: 2.5.0 adds the RASTER + CDP-rendered-font + IoU-text-less + systematic-pseudo layer. A new Node-side HARNESS (assets/diff/capture.mjs — playwright-core + odiff-bin) injects the unchanged analyze.js (MODE A reference / MODE B target) and ENRICHES its findings with three RENDERED signals a getComputedStyle dump structurally cannot give: (1) CDP RENDERED-FONT (CSS.getPlatformFontsForNode per visible text node — the genuinely-rendered familyName + isCustomFont; flags when live renders its loaded web font 'Inter Medium' while the target falls back to the system 'Helvetica' EVEN THOUGH getComputedStyle font-family lists Inter first on both — the trustworthy font-class signal, deduped per root cause); (2) ELEMENT-SCOPED RASTER DIFF (odiff on per-element crops of both full-page screenshots — a same-size box with a high pixel-diff and no other finding is a missing decorative child / occlusion / paint difference the DOM passes are blind to; size-mismatch raster diffs corroborate a geometry finding; 12% threshold + antialiasing to avoid text-edge flood); (3) IoU TEXT-LESS PAIRING (remaining bare svg/icon/decorative nodes paired by bbox Intersection-over-Union >= 0.9 so they too are presence/raster-checked). analyze.js itself gains SYSTEMATIC PSEUDO-ELEMENT extraction: capture() records pseudoStyle per node (::before/::after content, border-width/-color, background/-image, box-shadow, position, border-radius) and a MODE-B pass compares it for EVERY paired element (not just illustrations / not only the fold). Chromium launches with --font-render-hinting=none for deterministic raster crops (documented: this does NOT make headless == a real browser; the CDP rendered-font check, not any width or raster %, is the font-class authority). Findings carry bbox-delta + odiff mismatch% + rendered-font (the diff-as-instruction shape) + a summary.layers block. Deps: odiff-bin@4.3.8 + playwright-core@1.61.1 (prebuilt binaries, no node-gyp, reuse the host chromium cache); run npm install in assets/diff (and in any project's copied .mockup-fidelity).
plugins._v2note: 2.3.0 NARROWS the over-broad illustration-internal exclusion in the single-script analyzer/differ (assets/diff/analyze.js): a product ILLUSTRATION / mockup card (the ReadinessMockup 'Morrow Vale Resources' card, a hero product-shot, a readiness/preview panel) carries PLACEHOLDER content — fake tickers, demo numbers, sample labels — that legitimately differs, but its STYLING is real and checkable. Previously the differ dropped illustration internals ENTIRELY as noise, so real defects went unchecked (the card's row label had letter-spacing normal vs live's -0.3px, and the card had NO border vs live's 1px #e5e9f0 on ::after). Now MODE B SUPPRESSES only the placeholder TEXT-content comparison for illustration internals (a different demo ticker/number is not a finding → routed to noiseExcluded.illustrationInternals) while STILL comparing their COMPUTED STYLE — border (incl. the folded ::after/::before fold), border-radius, box-shadow, background/colour, letter-spacing, font-size/weight/family, padding, and geometry — pairing internals across sides BY POSITION/STRUCTURE (not text) and deduping repeated identical mockup-row styles to a few rows + a [×N] summary. 2.2.0 added the FONT-METRIC / VERSION detector (font/rendered-width-ratio — the SAME family at the SAME size rendering a consistently different width ⇒ a different font VERSION/metrics). 2.1.0 added the RENDERED-GLYPH-SHAPE / font-feature-effectiveness detector (font/feature-ineffective). 2.0.0 added INTERACTION STATES (:hover/:focus overrides from document.styleSheets) and RESPONSIVE (per-width 390/768/1280 desktop→mobile layout-TRANSITION divergence).

plugins.name: create-luke-content
plugins.description: Write any content in Luke Rhodes' authentic voice (Diolog CTO and co-founder), conditionally routed through the right persona variant for the content type: LinkedIn posts and long-form blog articles (engagement-optimised, with a Diolog graphic concept), marketing content (announcements, release notes, landing copy, campaign emails), code reviews (severity-calibrated candour), informal-but-professional Slack messages, short-form content (tweets, comments, bios), and ADHD-targetted / book-audience writing (the conversational brilliant-colleague register). One non-negotiable base voice (calm, direct, technically fluent, quietly witty, no em dashes, no AI clichés) synthesised from Luke's raw writing corpus, five persona deltas layered on top, and a deterministic voice-lint with per-format advisory checks. Formerly create-luke-article. v2.3 ports the owner-review learnings from the Amy package: signature-phrase rations with a retired-list mechanism, distribution-not-formula rhythm, referent discipline, peer-audience floor, genuine-question-or-no-question closes, register fencing, and a self-narrating meta-label ban (lint-enforced). v2.3.2 refreshes the bundled AI-signs field guide with the July 2026 empirical research layer. v2.3.3 syncs the field guide's once-per-document epigram/contrast rule. v2.4 syncs the repeated-phrase lint check and the updated field guide (countable texture budgets, scaffold-slot trap). v2.4.1 field-guide sync. v2.4.2 lint pattern sync.

plugins.name: create-amy-content
plugins.description: Write any content in Amy Benson's authentic voice (Diolog Founder & CEO), routed through eight persona variants: LinkedIn posts and thought leadership, long-form articles/blog posts, marketing content (announcements, launch/landing copy grounded in the diolog.app copy she curated and aligned with the Diolog voice & audience guide), sales/partnership outreach email (cold intros, consultative follow-ups, objection handling, gracious declines), Diolog investor updates, Slack/chat messages, short-form, and speaking notes for panels/podcasts/pitches. One evidence-anchored base voice (warm first, sharp underneath, direct questions with low-pressure asks, spaced-hyphen habit, no em dashes, AU spelling) synthesised from ~5,000 words of her verbatim email, LinkedIn, Slack and interview corpus (bundled, redacted), with a deterministic voice lint carrying her measured stylometric fingerprint. Generated with the create-voice-persona factory. v1.3 folds in Amy's own editorial review: broken-up LinkedIn formatting, genuine-question closes, peer-audience floor, referent discipline, register fencing for marketing, plain offer phrasing, no meta-labels, and retired phrases (bricks/Rome, 'came up through') enforced by the lint. v1.4 refreshes the bundled AI-signs field guide with the July 2026 empirical research layer and encodes the research-backed guards (plain copulas, epigram budget, hook-transition bans) in the base voice and lint config. v1.4.1 syncs the field guide's once-per-document epigram/contrast rule. v1.5 syncs the repeated-phrase lint check and the updated field guide (countable texture budgets, scaffold-slot trap). v1.5.1 field-guide sync. v1.5.2 lint pattern sync. v1.6 adds the meetings corpus: ~150k words of Amy's live sales/discovery calls (2025-26, six segments, redacted) distilled into references/research/amy-meetings-corpus.md - spoken hedge hierarchy corrected (kind of / I guess / to be honest dominate; I'd say / I find demoted to interview register), signature live moves encoded (the honesty-ask, resonance checks, conscious-of-time wind-down, affirm-and-redirect, concede-and-credit, the origin set-piece, live analogy-making), and the motto register-fenced (interview/keynote evidence, never observed on calls). Machine-transcript caveat binding: lexicon and moves only, never mechanics.

plugins.name: create-voice-persona
plugins.description: Build a complete, reusable voice persona package for any person from examples of their real writing — the generalised factory behind the create-luke-content pattern. Mines a writing corpus into an evidence-anchored base voice (every rule traced to a quoted sample), layers purpose-tailored persona variants on top (LinkedIn posts as the flagship, grounded in bundled LinkedIn engagement deep research plus any user-supplied research; plus long-form, marketing, short-form, chat, email, code review, speaker notes), generates a per-person deterministic voice lint, and bundles a distilled 'Signs of AI writing' field guide (from Wikipedia's WP:AISIGNS) so everything the persona writes avoids every known AI tell. Delivers a ready-to-install create-X-content plugin skill or a portable docs folder. v1.1 adds a stylometric layer grounded in voice-replication research: word-count corpus grading, syntactic-fingerprint extraction (sentence rhythm, contractions, nominalizations) enforced as lint advisories, anchor-diversity and style/content-entanglement rules, NNG tone dials per variant, and long-form drift re-anchoring. v1.3 adds an owner-feedback maintenance loop (corrections as highest-grade evidence, fix-the-class discipline, retired-phrase mechanism) plus guards distilled from real owner reviews: signature-phrase rations, distribution-not-formula rhythm, referent discipline, audience-knowledge floor, register fencing, and a self-narrating meta-label tell. v1.4 adds the empirical research layer to the bundled AI-signs field guide (quantified structural tells from 2023-2026 corpus studies, the 99.6% expert-detection and AI Penalty evidence, structure-vs-texture discipline, epigram budgets, epistemic stance markers, flatten-and-reconstruct guidance) and threads the new guards through the package blueprint and self-check. v1.5 adds the feedback-disposition discipline (every review item applied, overruled with stated reason, or routed AND landed in the owner's ledger), the restatement sweep (a corrected spec updated everywhere it is stated), and the once-per-document epigram rule to the field guide. v1.6: blind-generalisation-test learnings - voice_lint.py gains a deterministic repeated-phrase check (once-per-document rule, with repeat_allowlist); the blueprint mandates accreting approved output as sample anchors (corrections alone cannot teach a voice); the field guide encodes countable texture budgets and the scaffold-slots-are-fabrication-traps corollary. v1.7 encodes the rulebook/casebook separation in the blueprint: ledgers are maintainer-facing and stay out of the drafting load path, rules stand alone with one generic illustration, and recognition of previously reviewed material is never announced nor a substitute for a fresh pass. v1.7.1 widens the structural negative-parallelism lint pattern to the 'X, never the Y' and 'not the X, but the Y' contrast families - pattern-level checks over phrase bans.

plugins.name: acceptance-e2e
plugins.description: Turn any web feature's requirements into an acceptance-criteria-traceable Playwright e2e UI suite. From a requirements source (an issue/ticket, a spec or plan*.md, or a description) + the running app, it enumerates every acceptance criterion into a traceability matrix, grounds selectors against the live page, authors specs in the PROJECT'S OWN harness that assert content/render correctness (a chart actually draws, a figure is actually sourced) rather than mere element presence, runs them with isolation-safe disposable data, and fixes the tractable product bugs the suite surfaces. Project-agnostic: it discovers the repo's harness, auth, tenant/context model, run command, and base URL rather than assuming any one stack. Ships a portable e2e-playbook (selector traps, isolation, outcome-assertion patterns, recurring bug classes, the run-twice discipline) plus an optional Diolog-specific worked example.

plugins.name: diolog-brand-voice
plugins.description: Three skills that write publish-ready, on-brand content in Diolog's brand voice (Australian investor-communication SaaS; 'the workspace for everything investor-facing'; tagline 'Disclosure, without doubt.'), all driven by one bundled brand-voice persona and gated by one deterministic voice-lint. create-diolog-article writes long-form articles / blog posts / thought-leadership across the investor-education, IR-best-practice, thought-leadership and announcement registers. create-diolog-marketing-copy writes website marketing copy and marketing emails (benefit-first heroes, feature-to-'so what' bullets, problem-callback CTAs, subject/preview/body emails). create-diolog-business-case writes buyer-facing ROI / justification documents for the CFO / Company Secretary / board (status-quo cost -> what changes -> proof/security/compliance posture -> measurable outcomes -> next step). Every skill hard-encodes the house rules: no em or en dashes (spaced hyphen only), Australian English, sentence-case headings (except product feature names), every statistic carries an inline (Source, Year) with zero fabrication, compliance confidence capped at 95%, no financial advice or guaranteed outcomes, the Diolog narrative arc, correct register selection, and critique the old way of IR never the reader. The deterministic diolog_voice_lint.py hard-fails on em/en dashes and banned/AI-tell phrases and advises on American spellings, over-claim/guarantee language and citation coverage. The fuller brand counterpart to the deployed diolog-content-writer / diolog-linkedin-writer generation prompts; personal-voice LinkedIn posts remain with create-luke-content.

plugins.name: ship-feature
plugins.description: End-to-end feature delivery conductor for your product. Takes ONE feature (inline description or a markdown file) and drives it from a bare idea to merged, tested, production code by orchestrating the existing skills in a single in-session flow: design-craft (represent the whole feature UI in the design-system elements/composites/mock-pages, grounded in the repo's design system — e.g. apps/web-design-system — plus an optional DESIGN.md design-language spec when provided or present, and any provided HTML/CSS/JS mock) → triage (→ docs/specs/spec-DIO-XXXX.md) → plan (→ docs/plans/plan-DIO-XXXX.md) → work (implement in an isolated worktree) → a deferred-work loop (re-work, or child triage+plan+work for extra phases, all on the SAME branch) → gap-fix (close remaining spec gaps in code) → acceptance-e2e (a comprehensive Playwright suite over every user flow/action/interaction/menu, run against the feature branch locally and fixed) → finalize (commit → rebase → merge into the integration branch → push → clean up worktree(s)). The conductor stays in-session so it keeps the accumulated feature context across every stage and leans on the on-disk spec/plan/mock artifacts as durable, resumable memory; parallelism lives inside each sub-skill's own Workflow fan-out. One feature = one branch = one worktree; the merge is behind a fail-closed pre-merge gate. Builds on the design-craft, feature-spec-pipeline (triage/plan/work/gap-fix), and acceptance-e2e plugins.

plugins.name: ship-fleet
plugins.description: Backlog-wide feature-delivery orchestrator. Surveys ALL remaining feature work in a product repo — LEDGER.md, docs/specs/spec-*.md, docs/plans/plan-*.md, untriaged briefs in docs/features-to-triage/, refined HTML mocks in design/mocks/html, deep research in docs/deep-research/ — reconciles any in-progress worktrees, then writes ORCHESTRATOR.md (a combined plan + ledger for everything left) and orchestrator-hierarchy.html (a visual dependency hierarchy) BEFORE any execution. It then drives the backlog to done by running the ship-feature skill per feature: dependency-ordered, up to 8 concurrent Opus runner agents, with mechanical plan-scoped coding optionally delegated to the Cursor CLI (composer-2.5) and verified/fixed by Opus, while the orchestrating session itself stays on the session model. Includes a preflight that checks the project's directory/file conventions (offering to clone diolog-team-files for CODING_PRACTICES.md / NEW_PROJECT_BEST_PRACTICES.md, gather stray feature briefs into docs/features-to-triage/, and validate the monolith layout against NEW_PROJECT_BEST_PRACTICES.md). Builds on the ship-feature plugin (and through it design-craft, feature-spec-pipeline, acceptance-e2e).

plugins.name: customer-deck-builder
plugins.description: Build a branded, print-ready customer proposal / pitch deck from Diolog's 9-slide template system. Fills company-agnostic templates with a specific client's identity, contacts, pricing and numbers (prompting with multiple-choice questions for missing details), keeps the IR-proposal spine, and can design new slides into the same system via design-craft. Outputs a single self-contained web page with pan/zoom (trackpad + controls, fit-deck / fit-slide / 100%) that prints to a vertical, paged PDF at native 1920×1080 slide size.

plugins.name: design-md-from-website
plugins.description: Generate a spec-compliant DESIGN.md for any live website from its real pages — the browser-driven, measure-first successor to design-md-from-screenshots. Given a URL it uses playwright-cli (isolated named sessions, parallel probe agents with rate-limit backoff, full-page screenshots + getComputedStyle-by-role measured tokens + a colour census) to MEASURE the design of the key pages (home, about, product/services, contact, and any investor hub/portal) instead of guessing hexes and fonts from a screenshot, then synthesises an authoritative DESIGN.md in the official google-labs design.md format (YAML token frontmatter + Overview/Colors/Typography/Layout/Elevation/Shapes/Components/Do's & Don'ts + per-token provenance + self-critique) and refines it into an intentional, upgraded identity.

plugins.name: customer-business-case
plugins.description: Generate a single printable A4-page executive business case in Diolog's house style from a customer's proposal deck. Uses the /design-craft skill against a bundled single-page DESIGN.md and a templated version of the Alfabs business case, with all copy in the Diolog brand voice (create-diolog-business-case). Produces one dense, print-clean page (problem, solution, value, quantified outcome, investment, next step) on a fixed 794x1123 A4 canvas that prints to exactly one page.

plugins.name: improve-prompt
plugins.description: Review and rewrite prompts, system prompts, agent instructions, CLAUDE.md files, and Claude Code skills against Anthropic's prompt-engineering guidance and the Karpathy coding-behaviour principles. Diagnoses the artifact with a structured rubric (clarity, examples, XML structure, output format, hallucination guards, consistency, guardrails, token weight, rule craft, and — for skills — trigger description quality and progressive disclosure), then delivers a surgical rewrite with per-change rationale. Bundles the full Anthropic prompting docs (best practices, Opus 4.8 guide, guardrail guides, four production use-case guides) and the Karpathy material three ways: the guidelines verbatim, worked before/after examples, and a prompt-craft dissection (karpathy-prompt-craft.md) that treats the guidelines as a model of rule-writing — principle anatomy (name, bold directive, checkable behaviour bullets, self-test), tradeoff clauses, imperative-to-verifiable transforms, ownership-scoped cleanup, working-if success signals, a cross-surface reuse pattern (one canonical rule text wrapped per surface), and a mergeable hardening block — which the skill applies to every prompt it improves.

plugins.name: lecturn-deck
plugins.description: Convert PowerPoint (.pptx) ⇄ the lecturn.deck/1 JSON slide schema, and author/validate that JSON, with a bundled normative spec and a stdlib-only Python two-way converter. Import a .pptx into an editable presentation → slides → positioned-element tree (a discriminated union: text, stat, chart, image, line, shape, table, group, widget, embed) over a fixed canvas — media extracted, inherited placeholder geometry resolved, every construct classified F0 native / F1 carried / F2 fallback / F3 flagged with no silent loss — or build a structurally valid .pptx back from a deck JSON (native tables/charts, embedded media, notes, verbatim re-emission of carried parts; native-only stat/widget/embed exported as an honest static projection). The XML parser is hardened for untrusted uploads (refuses DTD/entity for XXE/billion-laughs, caps decompression for zip bombs) without any third-party dependency. Bundles the full schema + PPTX crosswalk spec, a worked example deck, a specialist agent for convert-validate-verify loops, and the deckconv CLI (from-pptx / to-pptx / validate / inspect).

plugins.name: research-to-roadmap
plugins.description: Recursive deep-research pipeline with two tracks. Track R (product roadmap): grounds in the product's existing surface, authors strategic + high-volume-ideation + ~10 drill-down Gemini Deep Research prompts (exclusion blocks, analysis lenses, epistemic bounding), then synthesises the returned reports — via verdict ledgers, an evidence hierarchy, gates-track sequencing, and red-team discipline — into wave-based roadmaps with stable numbered, triage-ready feature entries. Track G (general): takes ANY topic, thought, or idea → one initial research prompt (via deep-research-prompt-creator) → pause for the user's Gemini run → 5–10 drill-down prompts from the returned report → pause → synthesis into a decision-ready document.

plugins.name: product-gap-analysis
plugins.description: Whole-product gap analysis for any project against any stated goal (v1.0, GA, a roadmap horizon, or the product's own stated intent) — 'where does this project stand and what's left?'. Surveys everything the repo has — status ledgers/orchestrator logs OR issue-tracker state, specs' deferral sections, untriaged briefs or roadmap docs, reference catalogues, the live app code, navigation/wiring, and the test inventory — via up to 7 parallel research agents, adapting the lens plan to whatever artifact classes exist. Cross-checks every status claim against git (ledgers lie; git is authoritative), reconciles contradictions with a fixed authority order, and synthesizes one comprehensive evidence-cited gap report stratified into seven layers (coherence gaps ranked by contradiction-with-the-product's-own-claims, genuinely-unbuilt features, deferred tails + shared substrates, security/legal release gates + dark-by-default workers, test debt, docs drift, live defects) ending in a prioritized P0/P1/P2 proposed-work-items table that seeds the repo's pipeline (briefs or tickets). Read-only: writes one report, never product code.

plugins.name: ux-craft
plugins.description: Book-grounded UX engine for implementing, mocking, and reviewing web and mobile UIs, layouts, and user flows, plus marketing and transactional emails. Operationalizes the UX canon — Krug (self-evidence, trunk test), Norman (affordances/signifiers/feedback), Garrett's five planes, Cooper's goal-directed design, Nielsen's heuristics, Wroblewski's mobile-first, Yablonski's Laws of UX, Weinschenk's behavioral science, Fogg/Cialdini/Eyal persuasion with a hard ethics gate, Tidwell patterns, Lidwell principles, Refactoring UI hierarchy tactics, Lean UX mock-to-learn — as three modes (Build / Review / Advise) over eight references: psychology-laws (research-cited mechanisms), review-playbook (multi-lens pass with severity honesty, intentional-vs-accidental deviation handling, evidence-format findings with pasteable fixes, and prompt-injection guards for reviewed content), flows-and-forms (GDS one-thing-per-page, validation timing, state machines, undo vs confirmation, wayfinding), mobile-ux (touch targets, thumb zones, iOS/Material platform grammar, RN notes), email-ux (two-second triage contract, client rendering constraints, transactional patterns, CAN-SPAM/GDPR compliance floor), ux-writing (microcopy patterns, tone matrix, Butterick typography), ai-product-ux (AI inputs/wayfinding/governors/trust/tuners with friction-to-risk calibration and prompt-injection-aware retrieval rules), and per-surface pre-ship checklists incl. WCAG 2.2 quick pass and an AI-feature gate. The UX brain to design-craft's visual hands: it owns whether the thing works for humans; design-craft owns whether it looks intentional. v1.1.1 adds two pre-ship checklist floors routinely forgotten in mocks: designed not-found/error routes (no dead ends) and legally required links (privacy/terms/cookie consent).

plugins.name: create-diolog-infographic
plugins.description: Create a print-ready, single-page A4 infographic in Diolog's brand design system to communicate one finding, comparison, or metric (a benchmark result, a cost or quality trade-off, a KPI, a 'we measured X' story). Themes everything from the live Diolog website design system (financial-broadsheet plus instrument-panel: Newsreader serif with one italic accent, Inter body, JetBrains Mono for tickers and data labels, navy ground used once, one blue accent, accent-bright for data on navy only, semantic colour always paired with a dot or sign, no em or en dashes, sentence case, no second hue, no emoji, no hype). Writes any copy in Luke's voice via create-luke-content and runs its voice lint. Embeds the three brand fonts inline as base64 so the page is self-contained for print and for publishing as an Artifact, and pulls the diolog droplet mark plus lowercase wordmark. Charts a finding honestly (equal-length tracks with differing fills for proportions, x versus y with both values shown and the winner styled, one dominant focal number, never a comparison of success or task counts) and verifies the page renders and fits exactly one A4 page (~794x1123px at 96dpi) by serving over http, rendering with a cache-busting URL, measuring the page height, confirming the fonts loaded, and trimming until it fits. Leans on design-craft and ux-craft for visual and UX craft. Use whenever someone wants an infographic, a one-pager, a shareable stat graphic, a poster of a result, or a designed chart of data in the Diolog look, even if they only say 'turn these numbers into something I can share'. Not for multi-page guides, briefings or whitepapers (create-diolog-guides), multi-slide decks (customer-deck-builder), email product mockups (email-mockups), or a full website or app UI (design-craft). v1.2 adds a mandatory Step 0 design layer (a spacing scale every value draws from, print-first type, a margin column sized to its widest glyph run, line-height 1 on display glyphs, optical nudges via transform not margin), a Looking Contract for verification (rendering a screenshot is not looking at one; ask each capture "what is wrong with this?" not "is this done?"; crop to components at DPR 2-3 because a page thumbnail cannot resolve the defect; a clean gate means no known defect is present and never means verified, and the two claims are reported separately), ink measurement via a baseline probe plus canvas TextMetrics rather than getBoundingClientRect boxes, named layout anti-patterns to grep for (space-between, flex:1, margin-top:auto), and routing of any multi-page deliverable to create-diolog-guides.

plugins.name: create-diolog-guides
plugins.description: Build a multi-page, print-ready A4 guide in Diolog's brand design system as one self-contained HTML file that scrolls as a document on screen, exports to a clean A4 PDF, and carries screen-only micro-interactions that never reach the print. Use whenever the deliverable runs longer than one page: a guide, briefing, playbook, handbook, whitepaper, explainer, report, or 'turn this markdown into something we can send investors'. Sibling of create-diolog-infographic, which lands one finding on one page; this one sustains an argument across twenty, which is a systems problem rather than a composition problem. Mandates a design layer before any page exists (a spacing scale, a column grid, one shared baseline, print-first type, motion tokens), shapes the document into three to five page archetypes rather than twenty one-offs, and gates each archetype through design-craft's per-unit critique before reproducing it. Renders approved company-voice copy verbatim and builds all imagery as live HTML and CSS rather than raster. Ships a zero-dependency Node CDP harness that measures ink rather than boxes: guide-qa (cap-height alignment via baseline probe and canvas TextMetrics, optical gutters, stretch voids, orphan wrap rows, spacing-scale adherence, cross-page baseline drift, clamped-box centre drift, Range-based widows, WCAG AA contrast with alpha blended up the ancestor chain, plus per-component crops at DPR 3), scope-lint (selectors that cross a component boundary, dead rules, colliding global classes), page-fit (A4 fit plus the collisions overflow:hidden conceals, footer overlap, computed font families), motion-check (print and reduced-motion resting-state safety, then mid-flight frame capture), and export-pdf (verifies the produced PDF's page count, sheet size, link annotations, and that no transient animation label leaked into the ink). Carries an explicit verification epistemics: a gate is downstream of the findings that motivated it, so a clean gate means no known defect is present and never means verified; rendering a screenshot is not looking at one; and motion bugs exist only in mid-flight frames. Leans on design-craft and ux-craft (both blocking) and on create-luke-content or create-amy-content when copy must be authored rather than rendered. Not for a single-page infographic or poster (create-diolog-infographic), multi-slide decks (customer-deck-builder), email mockups (email-mockups), or app UI (design-craft). v1.0.1 documents the mixed-size-initial optical-centring pattern (a larger serif initial beside smaller mono caps rides high when baseline-aligned; nudge it down by half its cap-height surplus via transform) and two DOM ink-measurement traps (text-transform:uppercase means the measured string is not the rendered one; getComputedStyle metrics differ per font family so crop and look when the numbers fight you) - noting that the cap-misalign gate does not cover decorative initials, a live instance of the gate-sees-only-what-it-was-configured-for thesis. v1.1.0 adds the evidence-based emphasis-and-legibility layer from the July 2026 typography deep research: a binding per-page emphasis budget (italics display-only - the heading accent plus one isolated pull quote; micro-labels mono roman at or above the 11px floor; uppercase only for labels of five words or fewer; bold 1-2 phrases per paragraph), hierarchy via size steps and white space rather than font styling, row-major ordering for grouped sequences, pull quotes isolated outside the body flow, and the rule that this evidence outranks a master file's ad-hoc design notes - motivated by the italics overload and hierarchy flattening Amy's review caught in the shipped 30-questions guide. v1.2 adds references/design-review.md - the reviewer-correction ledger and the receiver for design feedback routed from other skills, seeded with the 2026-07 guide review's design items (cover grid texture, vertical ToC, section-opener hierarchy, label legibility, back-matter-earns-its-place, use-bridge room, unstable background effects); open items are debts a guide may not ship against silently.

plugins.name: bench-qa
plugins.description: Quality-assurance automation for the diolog-swe-bench benchmark corpus. bench-task-judge: judge one benchmark task (difficulty band from measured pass rates, over-fit verifier detection via a mutation harness, contract-parity checks, verdict + concrete cited fixes + rescore-vs-re-run decision). bench-corpus-review: regenerate the whole-corpus task review document with per-task verdicts, summary counts, an action list, and a delta vs the previous review - designed so a cheaper model can run it. bench-task-author: author new behavioural and ui design benchmark tasks to the deepswe-task-author bar, hard-gated by the judge skill and a measured peer-trio mini-cull before a task may enter the bench.

plugins.name: create-diolog-content
plugins.description: Write any content in Diolog's company voice, routed through four register personas: long-form A4 guide copy (the master files create-diolog-guides renders), articles/blog posts (investor education, IR best practice, thought leadership, announcements), website + email marketing copy, and buyer-facing business cases. Successor to the diolog-brand-voice skills, rebuilt on the create-amy-content architecture: one evidence-anchored base voice (plain and literal beats clever, the skim test, epigram budgets, verbatim positioning beats, measured confidence shown never stated, spaced hyphen never em dashes, AU English) synthesised from the team voice guide, the live diolog.app copy, and Amy's line-by-line 2026-07 review of shipped AI-drafted long-form; a review ledger that compounds future corrections; the July 2026 empirical anti-AI-writing research layer; and a deterministic voice lint with per-register format budgets. v1.0.1 adds the stylometric fingerprint extracted from ~26k words of the live diolog.com.au corpus (marketing pages, non-legal help articles, all blog articles; AI-drafted resources excluded). v1.1 adds the revision protocol (item-by-item feedback disposition - applied/overruled/routed-and-landed - plus restatement and duplication sweeps) after the guide revision validated clean on copy rules but evaporated routed design feedback, left a stale spec restatement, and duplicated an epigram across a spread; ledger entries 10-12 record the classes. v1.2, from a blind generalisation test (clean-room generation on a fresh topic, class-blind adversarial review): seeded approved sample anchors with an approval-plus-clean-lint admission rule and an accretion loop; data-shaped colour counts as a claim; scaffold slots never filled with invented psychology; countable texture budgets replace vary-by-intention; lint gains the repeated-phrase check and repeat_allowlist; ledger entries 13-15. v1.3 separates rulebook from casebook: the review ledger is maintenance-only and out of the drafting/review load path, model-facing files carry each rule with at most one generic illustration (verbatim case history lives only in the ledger), and a fresh-eyes constraint forbids document recognition from framing or substituting for a rules pass - so the skill can be tested fairly on previously reviewed material. v1.3.1: role descriptions carry their object (compressed abstraction applied to people), rewrite-from-disk guard in the revision protocol, and the fingerprint section's stale 'no fingerprint yet' prose corrected. v1.4 makes the Amy lineage explicit: the positioning-beats library is now annotated with live confirmations from her sales calls (both taglines verbatim, 2026), gains her live-confirmed beats (human in the loop, not-a-replacement/superpower, the internal-auditor metaphor for the Compliance Guardian, disclosure risk vs AI risk), and fences the retired pre-Nov-2025 'investor chat repository' positioning against regeneration.
